{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City other_designation<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its other_designation as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (other_designation) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|ja|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites of Interest|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=Kubebue Atagozuka, Taniguchi-yama, Mikurayama Kofun|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|series=Utsunomiya Archaeological Reports Vol. 37|publisher=Utsunomiya City Cultural Division|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[List of Japanese Kofuns]] * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}
== External Links == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - History and Culture of Utsunomiya (Utsunomiya Historical Resources Association) * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html Toyosato Mahoroba Road: Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - Utsunomiya Education Center

== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} (1 minute).

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} (1 minute).

== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} (1 minute).

== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.

Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} (1 minute).

= }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}

barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

= Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|ja|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites of Interest|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=Kubebue Atagozuka, Taniguchi-yama, Mikurayama Kofun|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|series=Utsunomiya Archaeological Reports Vol. 37|publisher=Utsunomiya City Cultural Division|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[List of Japanese Kofuns]] * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - History and Culture of Utsunomiya (Utsunomiya Historical Resources Association) * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html Toyosato Mahoroba Road: Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - Utsunomiya Education Center

chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|ja|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites of Interest|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=Kubebue Atagozuka, Taniguchi-yama, Mikurayama Kofun|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|series=Utsunomiya Archaeological Reports Vol. 37|publisher=Utsunomiya City Cultural Division|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[List of Japanese Kofuns]] * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - History and Culture of Utsunomiya (Utsunomiya Historical Resources Association) * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html Toyosato Mahoroba Road: Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - Utsunomiya Education Center

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).
