Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Taniguchi-yama Kofun | image = [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] | caption = View from the south | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō 1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|51.2|N|139|53|34.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | area = Diameter ~29 m × Height 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} (1 minute).

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 谷口山古墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 南側から |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |位置 = |緯度度 =36 |緯度分 =35 |緯度秒 =51.2 |経度度 =139 |経度分 =53 |経度秒 =34.3 |ISO =JP-09 |形状 = [[円墳]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |規模 = 直径 約29 [[メートル|m]]×高さ 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |出土品 = [[人骨]]、[[直刀]]、[[鉄鏃]]、[[耳環]]、ガラス玉、[[馬具]]<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=山田隆|work=広報うつのみや平成5年2月号|date=1993-02|publisher=宇都宮市|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 宇都宮市指定史跡<ref name="ku"/> |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |地図2 = |アイコン =円墳 |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} [[ファイル:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|石室の開口部]] '''谷口山古墳'''（やぐちやまこふん{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町に存在する[[円墳]]である{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。
== 概要 == 谷口山古墳は、直径約29メートル、高さ3.6メートルの円墳と推定されている{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。幅約0.5 - 1.1メートル、深さ約0.6メートルの周溝が確認されている{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}。[[駐車場]]の建設工事中に発見されたという経緯がある<ref name="ku"/>ため、墳丘はかなり削平されている{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。
横穴式石室を伴っており、発見当時は未開口であったことから出土した遺骨や副葬品は埋葬当時の形態を保持していたと考えられており、これをうかがい知る貴重な古墳とされる{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。2021年（令和3年）現在は、石室が開口した状態で公開されている{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}。
主な出土品は以下のとおり。
* 人骨3体分 * 直刀3口 * 鉄鏃3本 * 耳環3個 * ガラス小玉147個 * 馬具轡片1個
これらの出土品から、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている。
== 発見と発掘の経緯 == [[1991年]]（平成3年）2月下旬、インマヌエル宇都宮キリスト教会の駐車場の整備工事{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}中に発見された<ref name="ku"/>。古墳発見の報を受けた宇都宮市教育委員会文化課は墳丘・石室の調査を決定、[[2月25日]]の墳丘測量を皮切りに調査を開始した{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。4月12日から5月15日の調査中断をはさみ、[[6月1日]]には現地説明会を開催、[[7月8日]]に調査を終了した{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。
駐車場となる予定であったが、地権者の理解・協力を得て保存することになった{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。発見した地権者は「りっぱさに感動しました」と『広報うつのみや』に寄稿している<ref name="ku"/>。専門家による出土品の鑑定を経て<ref name="ku"/>、同年[[11月27日]]に宇都宮市指定文化財{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}（史跡<ref name="ku"/>）に指定された<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。横穴式石室が未盗掘かつ良好で、遺物の残存状況も良かったことから指定に至った{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。
== 周辺環境 == 谷口山古墳は宇都宮市北部の宇都宮丘陵の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] [[帝京大学]]・[[宇都宮美術館]]行き、ニュー富士見行きに乗車約15分、「緑ケ丘入口」下車徒歩5分、または玉生行き、宇都宮グリーンタウン行きに乗車約15分「豊郷中央小前」下車徒歩約15分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を北進約5分、もしくは[[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]の関堀町北交差点を西進約1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]沿い。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=うつのみや歴史探訪 史跡案内九十九景|date=2008-09-27|publisher=[[随想舎]]|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=久部愛宕塚古墳・谷口山古墳・御蔵山古墳|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|ncid=BA42543469|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71409|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書37|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会文化課|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ 谷口山古墳] - 宇都宮の歴史と文化財（宇都宮市歴史文化資源活用 推進協議会） * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html 豊郷まほろばの道―谷口山古墳―] - 宇都宮市教育センター

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[谷口山古墳]] to [[Yaguchiyama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 谷口山古墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:Yaguchiyama Kofun.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 南側から |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町1258-4{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |位置 = |緯度度 =36 |緯度分 =35 |緯度秒 =51.2 |経度度 =139 |経度分 =53 |経度秒 =34.3 |ISO =JP-09 |形状 = [[円墳]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |規模 = 直径 約29 [[メートル|m]]×高さ 3.6 m{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}} |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |出土品 = [[人骨]]、[[直刀]]、[[鉄鏃]]、[[耳環]]、ガラス玉、[[馬具]]<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=山田隆|work=広報うつのみや平成5年2月号|date=1993-02|publisher=宇都宮市|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 宇都宮市指定史跡<ref name="ku"/> |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |地図2 = |アイコン =円墳 |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} [[ファイル:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|石室の開口部]] '''谷口山古墳'''（やぐちやまこふん{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町に存在する[[円墳]]である{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。
== 概要 == 谷口山古墳は、直径約29メートル、高さ3.6メートルの円墳と推定されている{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。幅約0.5 - 1.1メートル、深さ約0.6メートルの周溝が確認されている{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}。[[駐車場]]の建設工事中に発見されたという経緯がある<ref name="ku"/>ため、墳丘はかなり削平されている{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。
横穴式石室を伴っており、発見当時は未開口であったことから出土した遺骨や副葬品は埋葬当時の形態を保持していたと考えられており、これをうかがい知る貴重な古墳とされる{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}。2021年（令和3年）現在は、石室が開口した状態で公開されている{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}。
主な出土品は以下のとおり。
* 人骨3体分 * 直刀3口 * 鉄鏃3本 * 耳環3個 * ガラス小玉147個 * 馬具轡片1個
これらの出土品から、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半と考えられている。
== 発見と発掘の経緯 == [[1991年]]（平成3年）2月下旬、インマヌエル宇都宮キリスト教会の駐車場の整備工事{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}中に発見された<ref name="ku"/>。古墳発見の報を受けた宇都宮市教育委員会文化課は墳丘・石室の調査を決定、[[2月25日]]の墳丘測量を皮切りに調査を開始した{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。4月12日から5月15日の調査中断をはさみ、[[6月1日]]には現地説明会を開催、[[7月8日]]に調査を終了した{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}。
駐車場となる予定であったが、地権者の理解・協力を得て保存することになった{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。発見した地権者は「りっぱさに感動しました」と『広報うつのみや』に寄稿している<ref name="ku"/>。専門家による出土品の鑑定を経て<ref name="ku"/>、同年[[11月27日]]に宇都宮市指定文化財{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}（史跡<ref name="ku"/>）に指定された<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。横穴式石室が未盗掘かつ良好で、遺物の残存状況も良かったことから指定に至った{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}。
== 周辺環境 == 谷口山古墳は宇都宮市北部の宇都宮丘陵の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北側の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]西岸に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] [[帝京大学]]・[[宇都宮美術館]]行き、ニュー富士見行きに乗車約15分、「緑ケ丘入口」下車徒歩5分、または玉生行き、宇都宮グリーンタウン行きに乗車約15分「豊郷中央小前」下車徒歩約15分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）長岡町交差点を北進約5分、もしくは[[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]の関堀町北交差点を西進約1分の[[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]沿い。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=うつのみや歴史探訪 史跡案内九十九景|date=2008-09-27|publisher=[[随想舎]]|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=久部愛宕塚古墳・谷口山古墳・御蔵山古墳|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|ncid=BA42543469|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71409|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書37|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会文化課|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ 谷口山古墳] - 宇都宮の歴史と文化財（宇都宮市歴史文化資源活用 推進協議会） * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html 豊郷まほろばの道―谷口山古墳―] - 宇都宮市教育センター

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus

** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).
