[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads

Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

[[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]]
'''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. 

== Overview ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.

The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.

== Discovery and Excavation ==
The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Access ==
; By Bus
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.

; By Car
* From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road):
** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* Human remains of three individuals
* Three iron swords
* Three iron arrowheads
* Three earrings
* 147 glass beads
* One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)

These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.

The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|ja|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites of Interest|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=Kubebue Atagozuka, Taniguchi-yama, Mikurayama Kofun|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|series=Utsunomiya Archaeological Reports Vol. 37|publisher=Utsunomiya City Cultural Division|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[List of Japanese Kofuns]] * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - History and Culture of Utsunomiya (Utsunomiya Historical Resources Association) * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html Toyosato Mahoroba Road: Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - Utsunomiya Education Center

{{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | burial goods = Human remains, iron swords, iron arrowheads, earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Takashi|work=Utsunomiya Monthly, February 1993|date=1993-02|publisher=Utsunomiya City|accessdate=2021-05-31}}</ref> | Satellite Barrows = | built = Late 6th century{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Heritage designation1 = Utsunomiya City Historic Site<ref name="ku"/> | designation1 = | Archeological Site ID = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | map = | type = Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Mount Tomatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|ja|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=Utsunomiya Historical Explorations: Ninety-Nine Sites of Interest|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=梁木誠・今平利幸|title=Kubebue Atagozuka, Taniguchi-yama, Mikurayama Kofun|date=1995-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71409|series=Utsunomiya Archaeological Reports Vol. 37|publisher=Utsunomiya City Cultural Division|page=78|ref={{sfnref|梁木・今平|1995}}}}
== Related Articles == * [[List of Japanese Kofuns]] * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E8%B0%B7%E5%8F%A3%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/ Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - History and Culture of Utsunomiya (Utsunomiya Historical Resources Association) * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/mahoroba16/html/yaguti/yaguti.html Toyosato Mahoroba Road: Taniguchi-yama Kofun] - Utsunomiya Education Center

Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.

earrings, glass beads, horse trappings<ref name="ku">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/014/535/08.pdf|title=谷口山古墳|author=Yamada Round barrow | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label = }} [[File:Yaguchiyama Kofun tomb entrance.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the stone chamber]] '''Taniguchi-yama Kofun''' (谷口山古墳{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=21}}{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}) is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}.
== Overview == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is estimated to have a diameter of approximately 29 meters and a height of 3.6 meters{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. A surrounding moat, about 0.5–1.1 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep, has been identified{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=27}}. The tumulus was significantly leveled during the construction of a parking lot where it was discovered<ref name="ku"/>.
The site features a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that was intact at the time of discovery. The chamber contained human remains and burial goods, maintaining their original burial configuration, making this tomb a valuable archaeological find{{sfn|塙|2008|p=112}}. As of 2021, the stone chamber is open to the public in its excavated state{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=112-113}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * Human remains of three individuals * Three iron swords * Three iron arrowheads * Three earrings * 147 glass beads * One piece of horse trappings (part of a bit)
These findings suggest the tomb was constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, during the latter half of the 6th century.
== Discovery and Excavation == The tomb was discovered in late February 1991 during the construction of a parking lot for the Immanuel Utsunomiya Christian Church{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Upon its discovery, the Utsunomiya City Board of Education's Cultural Division initiated a survey on February 25{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}. Excavation work began on April 12 and concluded on July 8, with a public site explanation held on June 1{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|p=23}}.
The site's landowner expressed deep admiration for the tomb's significance, contributing to its preservation rather than redevelopment<ref name="ku"/>. The site's excellent condition and the intact burial chamber led to its Historic Site as a Utsunomiya City designation1 (Historic Site) on November 27, 1991<ref name="ku"/>{{sfn|梁木・今平|1995|loc=序}}.
== Surrounding Environment == The Taniguchi-yama Kofun is situated in the southern area of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, along the western banks of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Tomatsuri-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496593}}. The region contains numerous other ancient tombs and archaeological sites, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135490863|1=Kitayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} ruins such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are connected by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} to Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art, or the New Fujimi line (15 minutes); alight at "Midori-ga-Oka Entrance," 5 minutes on foot. Alternatively, take the Tamanyu or Utsunomiya Green Town line to "Toyosato Central Elementary School" (15 minutes) and walk for 15 minutes.
; By Car * From National Route 119 (Utsunomiya Ring Road): ** Proceed north from Nagaokamachi Intersection (5 minutes), or west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection on the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1 minute).
